title: Shopping for clothes
description: Browse a local clothing shop — ask about sizes, try things on, and negotiate
location: Clothing shop, Mong Kok Ladies' Market area
context: HK has no shortage of clothes shops — from big mall chains to tiny street stalls in Mong Kok. In smaller shops, bargaining is expected: start below the asking price and expect to meet in the middle. Sizes are often labelled S/M/L or by waist/chest measurements.
culturalFact: In HK the phrase 平啲得唔得 (can you go cheaper?) is perfectly normal in smaller shops and market stalls. It's not rude — the vendor expects it. A polite smile and a laugh make the whole exchange friendlier.

lines.speaker: staff
lines.english: Welcome! What are you looking for?

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: ngo5 soeng2 wan2 gin6 T-shirt, sam1 sik1 gaa3.
lines.english: I'm looking for a T-shirt — something dark coloured.

lines.speaker: staff
lines.cjk: 呢邊有幾款，你睇下喜唔喜歡。
lines.jyutping: ni1 bin1 jau5 gei2 fun2, nei5 tai2 haa5 hei2 m4 hei2 fun1.
lines.english: We have a few styles over here — have a look and see what you like.

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 呢件幾好睇。有冇細碼㗎？
lines.jyutping: ni1 gin6 gei2 hou2 tai2. jau5 mou5 sai3 maa3 gaa3?
lines.english: This one looks nice. Do you have it in a small?

lines.speaker: staff
lines.jyutping: jau5, dang2 ngo5 lo2 bei2 nei5.
lines.english: Yes, let me get it for you.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: ho2 m4 ho2 ji5 si3 haa5?

lines.speaker: staff
lines.jyutping: si3 san1 sat1 hai2 jau6 sau2 bin1.
lines.english: The fitting room is on your right.

lines.speaker: you
lines.english: Can you do it a bit cheaper?

lines.speaker: staff
lines.cjk: 最平二百五十，係最低喇。
lines.jyutping: zeoi3 peng4 ji6 baak3 ng5 sap6, hai6 zeoi3 dai1 laa3.
lines.english: The lowest I can go is two hundred and fifty — that's the best price.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: hou2, ngo5 jiu3 laa3. m4 goi1.
lines.english: Alright, I'll take it. Thank you.
